codeReview.summary: Session read-cache behavior is mostly covered, but the mkdir optimization requirement for prepared workspaces is not implemented as specified and the new test asserts the wrong threshold.

issues.description: VAL-PERF-009/feature expectedBehavior requires that after one `ensureWorkspace`, 10 subsequent `saveSession` calls issue at most 2 total `mkdir` calls. The implementation still calls `mkdir(getSessionDir(...), { recursive: true })` on every save, and `saveSession` also triggers `renderSessionDocs` which calls two more `mkdir`s per save (`src/runtime/render.ts:92-93`), so mkdir calls still scale linearly with save count.

The new verification test contradicts the contract: it is named for the "at most two mkdir calls" requirement but asserts `expect(mkdirSpy.mock.calls).toHaveLength(30)`. This would pass even when the required optimization is not present, so the feature's required behavior is not validated.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker handoff marks `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`, but the delivered VAL-PERF-009 test does not match the literal contract threshold (≤2 mkdir calls). This suggests the current procedure is not being enforced tightly enough during assertion-coverage self-checks.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: core-worker SKILL.md step 7 requires literal assertion-coverage confirmation; handoff `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` is true; `tests/runtime/workspace-cache.test.ts:25` asserts 30 mkdir calls instead of ≤2 from validation-contract VAL-PERF-009.

summary: Review failed: the feature does add read-cache/invalidation and concurrency coverage, but it does not satisfy the prepared-workspace mkdir optimization requirement as specified, and its new test encodes the wrong success criterion.
